cost_note: single Claude Code subscription, <24h wall-clock for all games

ar25.desc: directional puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

bp35.desc: Gravity platformer: walk and fall to the gem; clicks remove single-use blocks or flip gravity.

cd82.desc: Navigation/maze solved through the synthesized code world model (predict-lookahead MPC).

cn04.desc: mixed puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

dc22.desc: Navigate a player to the goal via slidable crusher bridges, buttons, and color-cycle teleports.

ft09.desc: click puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

g50t.desc: Clone puzzle: record a path, an action-5 ghost holds a switch, then walk a fresh player to the goal.

ka59.desc: Directional navigation; a single click operates a door to complete the level.

lf52.desc: Peg/block-riding camera puzzle: rides and jumps move pegs across scrolling regions to merge a pair.

lp85.desc: Hungarian rings: click to rotate two interlocked rings until goal tiles align.

ls20.desc: directional puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

m0r0.desc: Mirror-merge: two horizontally-mirrored players; merge them onto a single cell.

r11l.desc: click puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

re86.desc: directional puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

s5i5.desc: Slide-to-goal: drive a tip riding a bar onto the goal cell.

sb26.desc: Program synthesis: click to fill subroutine slots so the flattened call tree equals a target color sequence.

sc25.desc: mixed puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

sk48.desc: Sokoban-as-snake: anchored snakes push colored blocks; match each pair's covered-color sequence.

sp80.desc: Interaction puzzle: the win fires on an accumulated interact (action-5) condition after a move sequence.

su15.desc: click puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

tn36.desc: click puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

tr87.desc: Glyph string-rewriting: edit output digits or rewrite rule sets (symbolic, not navigation).

tu93.desc: directional puzzle (mechanic not yet characterized in detail).

vc33.desc: Click-only puzzle: click the few valid sprite targets to satisfy each level.

wa30.desc: Sokoban variant: push boxes onto goals while autonomous helper/antagonist bots act each step.

protocol: offline executable-world-model recipe: deterministic env + unbounded resets; per-game source-faithful simulator + per-level search + deterministic replay-verify. Same full-game-completion metric as baseline1; we do not report RHAE (live action efficiency).
